channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Jerome Powell knows the Fed’s balance sheet got too big—Kevin Warsh has a plan, he just has to sell it without freaking out the markets
item.description: "I've got a lot of sympathy for Warsh's view. But the transition from here to where he wants to get, could be problematic."

item.title: The government’s free speech doctrine allows Trump to name things after himself
item.description: In November 2025, the Trump administration announced a special park pass commemorating the nation’s 250th anniversary that featured images of two pres...

item.title: Trump loves cheap gas—but a military conflict in Iran could nearly double your price at the pump
item.description: As Trump builds the biggest military force since 2003 outside Iran, the U.S. impacts could swing from $2.50 to $5 gasoline.

item.title: Ethics: A Manager Says They Won’t Hire the Child of Any Current Employee. Is That Going Too Far?
item.description: Does it make sense to have a blanket prohibition on any child of a current employee?

item.title: Why using facial recognition on your phone could leave you vulnerable
item.description: Biometric authentication—the ability to unlock your devices by using just your face or fingerprint—is one of the few smartphone features that, even to...

item.title: A Kid Snuck a Pond Into His Parents’ Yard—Now He Runs a $30M Fish Business
item.description: Here’s how one young man’s obsession with ponds has turned into a nine-store business servicing the likes of Jake Paul and Shaquille O’Neal.

item.title: This Olympic skill can boost your job performance
item.description: Olympians aren’t just physically exceptional—they’re masters at managing where their attention and energy go. Cognitive research finds a key link betw...

item.title: The Hidden Psychology Behind Why Great Packaging Sells More
item.description: Your product might be amazing, but if the packaging doesn’t match, you’re leaving money on the table.

item.title: This Kentucky Small Batch Bourbon Was Named Best in the World—And Costco Sells It for Under $50
item.description: The award-winning bourbon follows 1897 production rules.

item.title: New CBO report shows national debt spiraling into uncharted territory by 2035—and Trump’s tariff defeat will make the picture even worse
item.description: By 2035, the CBO expects the deficit to reach $2.96 trillion or 6.2% of GDP versus 5.8% today, and almost double the multi-decade, pre-pandemic averag...

item.title: This Performance Coach’s Simple Protocol Will Help You Sleep Like an Olympian
item.description: Follow these steps to get sleep that will give you caveman-like levels of energy—whether in the boardroom or on the Italian slopes.

item.title: Detroit automakers ask White House to be spared from new tariffs
item.description: The American Automotive Policy Council, which represents GM, Ford and Jeep-maker Stellantis, sent a letter to Trump’s trade team on Friday.

item.title: Trump team livid about Dario Amodei’s principled stand to keep the Defense Department from using his AI tools for warlike purposes
item.description: Anthropic might lose its $200 million Defense Department contract after the company questioned how its AI model was used during the Maduro raid.

item.title: Winter Olympics 2026 highlights: All the viral and dramatic moments—from the ‘Quad God’ to Alysa Liu
item.description: The Milan Cortina Winter Olympics may be winding down, but the memories will linger for years to come. The competition began on Wednesday, February 4,...

item.title: Why it’s so refreshing that Olympic champion Alysa Liu, who quit skating at 16, says she didn’t ‘need’ a gold medal
item.description: Alysa Liu, who quit skating at 16, didn’t “need” a gold medal, she told reporters in Milan—she had already found joy. The now-20-year-old from Califor...
